text:
Ortho-substituted phenyl rings are among the most common ring systems in organic chemistry: over 300 commercial drugs and agrochemicals are known to contain this motif. During the past decade the field of medicinal chemistry has entered what we refer to as “flatland” – where bioisosterism is explored as a method to overcome chemical challenges to drug development such as improve druglike properties of lead compounds and as a route to secure patent protection. All these recent efforts, however, are primarily directed toward producing para substituted analogous bioisosteres.

Saturated bioisosterics: Synthesis, characterization and application of 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes Herein, we report the synthesis, structural characterization and applications of a new class of saturated bioisosterics, namely 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes that serve as bioisosterics for the ortho-substituted phenyl ring. Crystallographic data confirmed that the bicyclic arrangement of these compounds retains similar exit vector conformations as compared to the aromatic counterparts. Using the established fungicides fluxapyroxad and boscalid (BASF) as leads, incorporation of 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes as bioisosterics results in significant increase in the water solubility by up to 10-fold, reduction in lipophilicity, while retaining excellent bioactivity. This work exemplifies an efficient way to modify the physicochemical properties of bioactive molecules, which is widely encountered in drug and pesticide development.
